codeReview.summary: The fix commit directly addresses the prior blocking failure by adding aggregate-composition flags (`hasEstimatedContributions`) in both DataStore and UsageStore summary builders, then wiring `hasEstimatedData` to that flag for provider and model summaries. Reviewing both diffs shows the original dominant-confidence behavior introduced in `bc71bff` was retained for `provenanceConfidence`, but the new mixed-composition signal now prevents mixed exact+estimated totals from being represented as fully exact in the `hasEstimatedData` contract path that previously failed.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The fix handoff marks `skillFeedback.followedProcedure=true`, but required context-read evidence is still missing from verification commands.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: `/Users/dewclaw/.factory/missions/76dfbf36-62ab-43ad-899f-b8b8f9e47932/handoffs/2026-04-09T16-28-08-219Z__m4-fix-provider-summary-mixed-provenance-semantics__9c9f735f-f0cd-4d3e-9302-e2c888867fd6.json` shows only `xcodebuild test` and `xcodebuild build` in `verification.commandsRun`; `.factory/skills/token-accounting-worker/SKILL.md` requires concrete context-read command evidence before setting `followedProcedure=true`.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Shared library docs appear mission-misaligned and can misdirect workers on this token-accounting milestone.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: `.factory/library/architecture.md`, `environment.md`, and `user-testing.md` are primarily account-switcher focused (browser/CLI profile switching) rather than token-accounting/reconciliation behavior under review, while this feature and milestone target provenance/reporting reconciliation paths.

summary: Pass: the fix resolves the prior mixed-provenance semantics failure by introducing explicit aggregate estimated-contribution tracking across both summary paths and validating mixed/all-exact/derived-exact scenarios with new tests.
